svg.title: Recruitment agent system — production implementation with decision points and HITL gates called out
svg.desc: Inputs row (grey, human-authored). Pipeline stages 1-6 in the middle, with explicit decision outputs and HITL badges. Orchestrator (left band) drives every stage; Graphiti (right band) is the substrate. Outcomes feed the nightly learning loop.
svg.text: decision outputs called out at every gate · HITL badges where a human is in the loop · orchestrator drives stages · Graphiti is shared substrate

g.text: Teal — sub-agent that produces a score
Orange — deterministic engine or rule gate (no LLM)
Grey — human input, action, integration, or data event

svg.text: STAGE -1 · PRE-BASELINE — ONE-SHOT BACKFILL OF HISTORICAL CANDIDATES BEFORE THE LIVE CYCLE STARTS

g.text: runs once per role at onboarding — never on live applicants
Walks the role's Workable history: every application that reached phone_screen / technical_interview / offer / hired,
plus a sample of explicit rejections, gets replayed through the same sub-agents the live cycle uses.
→ pre_screen · cv_scoring · graph_priors run on each historical CV with valid_from = application.created_at,
learned_at = now() (bi-temporal — we do not claim the agent scored them at the time).
→ Synthetic HiringOutcome episodes emitted (reached_interview / received_offer / hired / rejected_late) so the
nightly policy fitter sees real labelled paths per-role first, with √n pooling toward the org baseline when role data is thin.

svg.text: writes AgentScoreEvent + HiringOutcome episodes into Graphiti

g.text: STAGE 0 · INPUTS THAT FEED EACH CYCLE — ALL HUMAN-AUTHORED / INTEGRATIONS
STAGE 1 · CHEAP SCORERS IN PARALLEL · 1 PRODUCES A BRANCHING DECISION
Decides whether to send a take-home task, skip it (the candidate already has equivalent artifacts),
or ask for artifacts (no calibrated template for this role yet — admit unknown rather than guess).
When the role has send_assessment_requires_approval=True, send_task pauses for recruiter approval (HITL).
STAGE 3 · ASSESSMENT SCORING (ASYNC ON SUBMISSION) — ONLY IF TASK SENT
Grades the candidate's submitted task against the role's rubric.
Async — fires on submission, decoupled from the main cycle. Returns per-rubric breakdown,
STAGE 4 · POLICY COMPOSITION — THE FOUR-WAY DECISION
Combines the four sub-agent scores into one of four recommendations.
Logistic regression with √n hierarchical pooling + isotonic calibration — pre-pilot appropriate.
Abstention fires when per-agent uncertainty ≥ 0.5, max-median spread > 0.5, or confidence < 0.6.
STAGE 5 · RECRUITER REVIEW — PRIMARY HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP GATE
three-button surface · Hub queue + TeachModal v2
Recruiter sees the recommendation + evidence and picks one of three responses.
fires the action via the shared action layer; recruiter is actor of record
recruiter does something different manually; decision stamped with attribution
reverts to pending with structured correction; org-scope teach needs second-admin co-sign
Graph-writeback hints: low-risk auto-commit, medium-risk co-sign queue, protected attributes blocked.
The single path through which candidate-facing actions actually happen.
send_assessment can pause for recruiter approval when the role's HITL toggle is on.
REALISED OUTCOMES — FEED THE LEARNING LOOP
What actually happened to the candidate after the decision.
reached_interview · received_offer · hired · rejected_late · withdrew. Written to Graphiti as
HiringOutcome nodes linked via RESULTED_IN to the original DecisionEvent.
NIGHTLY LEARNING LOOP · READS FROM GRAPHITI · PROMOTION REQUIRES HITL CO-SIGN
